tensions.actual: Technically complete: 30 CLI commands, 9 MCP tools, Stripe billing, magic link auth, cross-platform binaries, self-hosting. 0 paying users. Email sent to Stripe projects.dev for partner listing. No distribution strategy, no content, no demo video. Auth UX needs upgrade to browser-based device code flow. AGENTS.md stale.
tensions.desired: Have 10 paying users on spikes.sh

tensions.actual: Multiple half-finished features, outdated docs, 26 stale GitHub issues
tensions.desired: Product is clean, polished, and finished

tensions.actual: Full onboarding flow verified and working end-to-end: local (init→inject→serve→feedback→list) and hosted (login via magic link email→share→share-link→shares). All technical blockers resolved. Magic link emails delivered via Resend from login@spikes.sh, verify landing page works, CLI poll detects verification. Only non-technical work remains: getting actual users.
tensions.desired: New user goes from discovery to first feedback to value with no confusion

tensions.actual: Fixed: removed phantom commands (dashboard), corrected CLI flags (list --type, export --output, inject flags), fixed env var names, updated MCP tool count and options, fixed install command
tensions.desired: Landing page and docs reflect only what's actually shipped

tensions.actual: Full flow verified end-to-end including hosted: init→inject→serve→feedback→list, login→share→share-link-live→shares-list all work. Fixed: port panic, auth.toml hints, README crate name, crates.io published (0.3.2), update command uses GitHub releases, D1 migrations applied (prod+staging), added GET /auth/poll to worker for CLI login, deployed. Remaining gap: magic link email not sent (no email service configured), workaround is --token flag.
tensions.desired: Install → first feedback → value moment is smooth and documented

tensions.actual: Device code flow works end-to-end but confirm step only asks for email without verifying it. Need: browser session cookie for instant confirm when already authenticated, email verification fallback for first-time users. Hybrid approach (option 3).
tensions.desired: Browser-based CLI auth flow (device code pattern)

Actual submissions blocked on user GitHub device-code auth and outreach.
tensions.desired: Get listed in MCP directories (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf)

tensions.actual: handleDevicePage has setTimeout(sessionConfirm, 500) — logged-in victims sent to attacker's code URL get auto-confirmed, handing attacker a bearer token
tensions.desired: Device confirm requires explicit user click, no auto-submit

tensions.actual: handleVerifyPage reuses CLI bearer token verbatim as spikes_session cookie; handleDeviceEmailVerify already does the right thing (mints separate token) — paths are inconsistent
